paper_title: Design and modeling of linearly-constrained compliant spines for human-scale locomotion on rocky surfaces
source_file: 机理文献/核心文献/07_ Design and modeling of linearly-constrained compliant spines for human-scale locomotion on rocky sur.pdf
sections_checked: 3.1 Spine array adhesion with varying loading angle
Appendix D Opposed gripper model derivations

equations_checked.notes: Visually verified and retained as E1. Numerator mu*cos(psi), denominator cos(psi)-mu*sin(psi), multiplied by F_P. Interpreted only for the friction-limited, pure-shear case shown in Fig. 5; asperity strength truncates the divergence near the friction cone.
Visually verified and retained in E2. The threshold is psi <= beta + pi/2 - alpha/2. The coefficient c_i is not numerically reported in this paper and is intended to be fitted from angular data.

Visually verified and retained in E2. The paper sets C_c=1 inside beta-atan(mu_s) <= phi <= beta+atan(mu_s) and prints one multiplicative slip expression outside. Separate upper/lower out-of-cone branches are described but not fully written as a complete piecewise function.
Visually verified and retained as E3: s = l/(l-l_e) times epsilon. The reported backlash-induced inclination change is within 2 degrees in most cases and is ignored by the paper.

Visually verified and retained as E4. Eq. (6) uses the minimum over Phi_{M_i}/k+S_i; Eq. (7) uses k*max(X-S_i,0); Eq. (8) integrates over failure mode, maximum-force and backlash distributions. The distribution parameters are not fully listed in this paper.

Visually verified and retained as E5. The usable-slope density is linear over delta psi; Eq. (10) normalizes the angular integral by the phi=0 value; Eq. (11) defines the usable slope bounds; Eq. (12) gives beta'=asin(sin(beta)cos(theta)). Isotropy and the high-slope linear-density approximation are preserved as warnings.
Visually verified and retained as E6. The denominator cos|phi|-mu_t*sin|phi| must be positive. The small spine-extension spring force is explicitly omitted.

Visually verified as the sum of four tile limit surfaces after coordinate rotations. Omitted from the compact formula set because it is specific to the four-tile SupraPed staff geometry and does not add a new local contact or paired-array law.

The printed symbols and coefficients were visually verified. Eq. (17)-(18) are retained as E7 only to preserve the taper-angle and worn-diameter trend. The printed circular-section factors in Eq. (16)-(17) do not match the usual second moment of area for a solid circular beam, so direct numerical use is rejected pending re-derivation.

Visually verified as the trapped-particle force balance and friction requirement. Omitted because particle-jamming material coefficients and particle-size distributions are not sufficiently characterized for the current solver; the qualitative anti-jamming requirement is summarized textually.

Visually verified and retained as E8. The two mirrored tile limits are evaluated at f_y/2 and shifted by f_p through f_x1=f_p+f_x and f_x2=f_p-f_x; either negative/zero tile capacity collapses the mechanism limit to zero. Outer and inner hard-stop branches were checked exactly as printed.

figures_checked.reason: Humanoid/staff application context; no irreducible mechanism or quantitative evidence.
The comparison of long-flexure and linearly constrained spines is adequately preserved by the compact mechanism description and parameter table.
Spine-strength failure locations are preserved textually and through Eq. (16)-(18); the numerical formulas carry an explicit coefficient warning.
Irreplaceable geometry for the slip, tip-contact, shaft-contact and channel-slip regimes and for the alpha/beta/phi/psi angle definitions. Cropped from a 300-dpi page render and visually verified.
The three experimental regimes, parameters and five trials per slope are fully represented in V1; the plot adds no reusable exact parameter.
The 150-trial empirical distribution and truncated-Gaussian asperity-failure portion are summarized; the paper does not supply enough fitted parameters for direct reuse.
Minimal combined simulation-experiment evidence for spine-count saturation, 0.1/0.5 mm backlash and fine/coarse concrete variability. Cropped from a 300-dpi page render and visually verified.
The 300-load 3D limit-surface validation is retained as V3; Fig. 7 and Fig. 12 were prioritized to cover the independent array-scaling and opposed-array mechanisms within the three-image limit.
The effective-friction trend is represented exactly by Eq. (14) and its positivity condition.
Opposed rails, preload springs and hard stops are compactly described and modeled more precisely by Eq. (25)-(28).
The opposed 3D surface is a computed visualization; the experimentally validated cross-section in Fig. 12 is stronger minimal evidence.

Direct model-experiment comparison showing both the single-tile adhesion-limit branch and outer-hard-stop branch. Cropped from a 300-dpi page render and visually verified with complete axes, units and legend.
Humanoid support-region application result; not required for the contact/array/paired-grasp model.
Four-tile smart-staff geometry is application-specific and Eq. (15) was omitted from the compact core.
Application-specific effective-friction curves are summarized by Eq. (14) and selected parameter examples.
Floating-tile tendon-pulley architecture is described in M6/V5; the mechanism photograph is not needed for solver input.
The perfect-inter-tile-load-sharing calculation and diminishing tile-count benefit are retained as a trend only; no portable closed-form parameters are given.
Coordinate and parameter definitions are distributed across Fig. 4 and the formula-variable entries; retaining both would be redundant.
Particle-jamming force balance is omitted from the compact solver evidence because its material/particle statistics are unavailable.
The mirrored-limit offset construction is preserved algebraically by Eq. (25)-(26); Fig. 12 supplies the experimental counterpart.
Preload trend is summarized with its 5/10/15/20 N comparison and non-monotonic warning; Eq. (25)-(26) is more reusable.

omitted_sections: General humanoid, climbing-technology and robot-history background in the Introduction

uncertain_items.detail: The equations were transcribed exactly as printed, but their section coefficients do not match the usual second moment of area for a solid circular beam. Trend use is retained; numerical use requires re-derivation from the actual spine cross-section.

The friction-cone interval and one reduction expression are printed, while distinct expressions for both upper and lower out-of-cone directions are not fully enumerated. A robust implementation must re-derive both branches.

uncertain_items.item: Monte Carlo input distributions and c_i
uncertain_items.detail: The paper gives the solver structure but not a complete numerical specification of the failure-mode mixture, maximum-force distributions or fitted c_i. It explicitly refers to Wang et al. (2016) for further distribution details.

uncertain_items.location: PDF p.4, Section 2.2.3 and Appendix C
uncertain_items.detail: The narrative switches among spine-particle, channel-particle and prototype material friction values. Because particle size/distribution is also missing, the numerical jamming relation was not retained as a solver formula.

Part of the 3D limit-surface display is mirrored under an isotropy/symmetry assumption. The evidence card treats the figure as an average directional validation, not a complete independently sampled surface.

known_warnings: Linearly constrained spines have negligible tangential search travel and are not mechanically equivalent to independently spring-supported spines with long search strokes.
The surface model uses an isotropic, high-slope linear probability density and does not provide a measured three-dimensional roughness field.
The array model assumes equal contact stiffness, uniform random backlash and a common travel stopped by the first spine reaching its random limit; dynamic redistribution is absent.
The paired-gripper model assumes symmetric tiles, equal lateral-force sharing and rotational constraint parallel to the surface; force/moment balance for an asymmetric free gripper is not solved.
Experimental standard deviation for the opposed limit is about 30 percent, so the mean limit surface is not a safety boundary.
The source PDF has a valid 15-page layout and all pages/equations/captions were visually inspected; no OCR-only formula was accepted.
character_count_method: Count of CJK Unified Ideographs after excluding display-formula blocks, Markdown table rows and image-link rows; within the allowed 3500-character extension because the paper contains four independent single-spine, array, 3D-limit and opposed-gripper submodels.
